task_completion.description: Open and read the habitat request document
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/gulfstream_habitat_request.txt.

task_completion.description: Load the NOAA OI SST dataset in Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, showing 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' in the Datasets/Sources panel and the 'sst' variable selected.

task_completion.description: Create a global SST plot for February
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a global map of sea surface temperature for February (time index 1), or a save dialog exporting this plot as 'sst_global_feb.png'.

task_completion.description: Create a regional plot of the Gulf Stream front
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing the SST map zoomed into the Northwest Atlantic region (25-50°N, 80-50°W), or a save dialog exporting this plot as 'gulfstream_front_feb.png'.
A text editor open showing the drafting of 'thermal_front_report.txt', containing temperature observations or analysis.

task_completion.description: Save all required files to the specified output directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the directory /home/ga/Documents/GulfStream containing exactly the three required files: sst_global_feb.png, gulfstream_front_feb.png, and thermal_front_report.txt.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to perform the analysis and generate plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show active interaction with the Panoply graphical interface (e.g., adjusting map projections, changing time indices, or using the export menu) rather than using Python/CLI scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The plots were genuinely rendered from the dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The plots must be visible within Panoply's internal plot viewer before being saved, proving they were generated from the provided NetCDF data and not downloaded or copied from elsewhere.

integrity.description: The report values are derived from actual data inspection.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen inspecting the data in Panoply (e.g., using the Array tab, hovering over the plot to see data values, or adjusting the scale) to write the report, rather than hallucinating temperature values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires analyzing the NOAA OI SST data file located at /home/ga/PanoplyData/sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc using the 'sst' variable. The analysis must be for February (time index 1). The regional analysis must focus on the Northwest Atlantic (25-50°N, 80-50°W). The final deliverables must be saved in /home/ga/Documents/GulfStream and include exactly three files: sst_global_feb.png, gulfstream_front_feb.png, and thermal_front_report.txt.
